article_title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Flaws in Identity Services Engine and Webex, Including a 9.8-Severity SSO Bypass

content_preview.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Flaws in Identity Services Engine and Webex, Including a 9.8-Severity SSO Bypass
content_preview.summary: Cisco discloses four critical vulnerabilities across ISE and Webex, with the most severe allowing unauthenticated attackers to impersonate any user via a broken SSO certificate check.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cisco released three critical security advisories on April 15, 2026, disclosing four vulnerabilities that affect two widely deployed enterprise products: the Webex collaboration platform and Identity Services Engine (ISE), the company's flagship network access control system. The most severe flaw, CVE-2026-20184, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to impersonate any user on a Webex deployment configured with single sign-on. Three additional ISE...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong infosec article on Cisco ISE/Webex critical vulnerabilities.
editor_notes.source_verification: Sources captured (CSO Online, The Hacker News). CVE-2026-20184 Webex SSO bypass CVSS 9.8, three ISE CVEs CVSS 9.9, ISE patch matrix 3.1-3.5, SAML cert rotation from captured snapshots.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: CVE IDs and CVSS scores consistent.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Cisco ISE/Webex vulnerability coverage.
